content.full_text:
OFFICES OF

THE COUNTY COUNSEL
County Of Orange

CLAYTON H. PARKER
CHIEF ASSISTANT

COUNTY ADMINISTRATION BUILDING • P.O. BOX 1379 • SANTA ANA, CA 92702 • 634-3300

JOHN M. PATTERSON
ARTHUR C. WAHLSTEDT, JR.
JOHN W. ANDERSON
ROBERT L. WILSON
VICTOR T. BELLERUE
JOHN R. GRIEST
CHARLES B. SEVIER
WALTER D. WEBSTER
IRVYNE C. BLACK
RALPH W. BENSON
ROBERT H. SAN CHEZ
KENNARD P. HART, JR.
DANIEL E. HANDY
JAMES R. FLOURNOY
TERRY C. ANDRUS
TERRY E. DIXON
EDWARD E. DURAN
JOY C. FISHER
FRANK J. PEIRETTI
BARBARA TAM THOMPSON
RICHARD D. OVIEDO

November 18, 1976

DEPUTIES

P-298

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Regina Kutchins

Re: Government Code Section 81005(g) and
2 Cal. App. 18115(a)

Dear Ms. Kutchins:

Pursuant to our telephone conversation of this date, this
letter will serve to confirm our interpretation of the above-
referenced status and regulation.

The above-referenced Code section states that reports required
by Chapter 7 shall be filed with the "agency" provided that reports
filed by the head of the agency shall be forwarded to the code review-
ing body and a copy retained by the agency. Fair Political Practices
Commission Regulation 18115(a), however, states as follows:

"Agencies which receive statements of econo-
mic interests shall:

(a) Within five (5) days following the dead-
line for filing such statements forward each ori-
ginal statement to the code reviewing body indi-
cating the date received on the face of the state-
ment, and retain a copy. . . ."

You informed the undersigned that this regulation is not being
interpreted by the Commission in a manner that would change the duties
and responsibilities set forth in Section 81005(g) but merely means
that those reports which are required to be forwarded to the code re-
viewing body (such as reports of the heads of the respective agencies)
must be so forwarded within five (5) days. Reports of other employees
of the agency need not be forwarded to the code reviewing body but
remain on file with the agency.

I can see this is a mostly blank page with only two small circular objects at the top (which appear to be decorative elements or page markers) and what might be a small mark on the left side of the page. The rest of the page is empty white space.

Since there is no readable text content to transcribe from this document, there is nothing to transcribe.

Fair Political Practices
Commission
November 18, 1976
Page Two

Thank you for your kind assistance.

Very truly yours,

ADRIAN KUYPER, COUNTY COUNSEL


By ________________
Terry C. Andrus, Deputy

TCA:gr

BOX 1379 • SANTA ANA, CA 92702 • 834-3300 November 18, 1976 Fair Political Practices Commission 1100 K Street Sacramento, California 95814 Attention: Regina Kutchins Re: Government Code Section 81005(g) and 2 Cal. App. 18115(a) Dear Ms. Kutchins: Pursuant to our telephone conversation of this date, this letter will serve to confirm our interpretation of the above-referenced statute and regulation. The above-referenced Code section states that reports required by Chapter 7 shall be filed with the "agency" provided that reports filed by the head of the agency shall be forwarded to the code reviewing body and a copy retained by the agency. Fair Political Practices Commission Regulation 18115(a), however, states as follows: "Agencies which receive statements of economic interests shall: (a) Within five (5) days following the deadline for filing such statements forward each original statement to the code reviewing body indicating the date received on the face of the statement, and retain a copy..." You informed the undersigned that this regulation is not being interpreted by the Commission in a manner that would change the duties and responsibilities set forth in Section 81005(g) but merely means that those reports which are required to be forwarded to the code reviewing body (such as reports of the heads of the respective agencies) must be so forwarded within five (5) days. Reports of other employees of the agency need not be forwarded to the code reviewing body but remain on file with the agency. The image you provided is completely blank, with no visible text, numbers, or any other content. It appears to be an empty page or a page with no content added yet. If you have another page or section of the document that you would like me to review, please provide it. Fair Political Practices Commission November 18, 1976 Page Two Thank you for your kind assistance. Very truly yours, ADRIAN KUYPER, COUNTY COUNSEL By [signature] Terry C. Andrus, Deputy TCA:gr
